concepts.title: Order of operations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The agreed sequence (parentheses, exponents, multiplication/division, addition/subtraction) for evaluating an arithmetic or algebraic expression.

concepts.title: Evaluating algebraic expressions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Substituting numeric values for variables in an expression and simplifying to a single value.

concepts.title: Combining like terms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Simplifying an expression by adding or subtracting terms that share the same variable and exponent.

concepts.title: The distributive property
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Multiplying a single term across a sum, a(b+c) = ab+ac, used to expand or factor expressions.

concepts.title: One-step equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving an equation that requires a single inverse operation to isolate the variable.

concepts.title: Two-step equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving linear equations that require two inverse operations applied in the correct order.

concepts.title: Equations with variables on both sides
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving linear equations where the unknown appears on both sides by collecting variable terms together.

concepts.title: Solving linear inequalities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving inequalities like a single linear equation, flipping the inequality sign when multiplying or dividing by a negative.

concepts.title: Solving for a variable (literal equations)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rearranging a formula to isolate a chosen variable in terms of the others.

concepts.title: Rates and unit conversion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using ratios and dimensional analysis to convert between units and reason about rates like speed.

concepts.title: The coordinate plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Plotting ordered pairs (x, y) on perpendicular axes to locate points in two dimensions.

concepts.title: Slope of a line
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The steepness of a line measured as rise over run, the constant rate of change between any two points.

concepts.title: x- and y-intercepts
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The points where a graph crosses the x-axis (y=0) and the y-axis (x=0).

concepts.title: Graphing linear equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Drawing the straight-line graph of a linear equation using slope and intercepts or a table of points.

concepts.title: Slope-intercept form
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a line as y = mx + b, where m is the slope and b is the y-intercept.

concepts.title: Point-slope form
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a line as y - y1 = m(x - x1) from a known point and slope.

concepts.title: Standard form of a line
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a line as Ax + By = C and converting between standard and slope-intercept forms.

concepts.title: Parallel and perpendicular lines
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recognizing that parallel lines share a slope and perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals.

concepts.title: Solving systems by graphing
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the solution to a system of linear equations as the intersection point of their graphs.

concepts.title: Solving systems by substitution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving one equation for a variable and substituting that expression into the other equation.

concepts.title: Solving systems by elimination
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Adding or subtracting equations to eliminate a variable and solve the system.

concepts.title: Graphing two-variable inequalities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Shading the half-plane of solutions to a linear inequality with a boundary line.

concepts.title: Systems of inequalities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the overlapping shaded region that satisfies several linear inequalities at once.

concepts.title: What is a function?
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A rule assigning exactly one output to each input, often shown as a mapping or with the vertical line test.

concepts.title: Function notation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using f(x) notation to name a function and evaluate its output for a given input.

concepts.title: Domain and range
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The set of valid inputs (domain) and the set of resulting outputs (range) of a function.

concepts.title: Interpreting function graphs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reading features such as intercepts, increasing/decreasing intervals, and maxima from a function's graph.

concepts.title: Arithmetic sequences
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sequences with a constant difference between terms, expressed with recursive or explicit formulas.

concepts.title: Geometric sequences
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sequences with a constant ratio between terms, the discrete basis of exponential growth.

concepts.title: Absolute value functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Graphing the V-shaped function y = |x| and its transformations.

concepts.title: Piecewise functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Functions defined by different rules over different intervals of the domain.

concepts.title: Properties of exponents
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rules for multiplying, dividing, and raising powers, including negative and zero exponents.

concepts.title: Radicals and square roots
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Simplifying square roots and other radicals, including products and quotients of radicals.

concepts.title: Scientific notation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing very large or small numbers as a coefficient times a power of ten.

concepts.title: Exponential functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Functions of the form y = a·b^x whose graphs show rapid growth or decay.

concepts.title: Exponential growth and decay models
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Modeling real situations like compound interest or population with exponential growth or decay factors.

concepts.title: Multiplying binomials (FOIL)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Expanding the product of two binomials using the distributive property (FOIL) to form a quadratic.

concepts.title: Factoring quadratics
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rewriting a quadratic trinomial as a product of two binomials.

concepts.title: Special products: difference of squares & perfect squares
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Recognizing patterns like a²-b² and (a±b)² to factor or expand quickly.

concepts.title: Graphing parabolas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The U-shaped graph of a quadratic function, defined by its vertex, axis of symmetry, and direction of opening.

concepts.title: Solving quadratics by factoring
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the zero-product property on a factored quadratic to find its roots.

concepts.title: Completing the square
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Rewriting a quadratic as a perfect-square trinomial plus a constant to solve it or find the vertex.

concepts.title: The quadratic formula
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The formula x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac))/2a that solves any quadratic, with the discriminant indicating the number of real roots.

concepts.title: Vertex form of a quadratic
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing a quadratic as y = a(x-h)² + k to read its vertex (h, k) directly and graph transformations.

concepts.title: Rational vs. irrational numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Distinguishing numbers expressible as fractions from non-repeating, non-terminating decimals like √2 and π.

concepts.title: Rigid transformations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Translations, rotations, and reflections that move a figure without changing its size or shape.

concepts.title: Translations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sliding every point of a figure the same distance and direction in the plane.

concepts.title: Rotations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Turning a figure about a fixed center point by a given angle.

concepts.title: Reflections
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Flipping a figure across a line of reflection to produce a mirror image.

concepts.title: Dilations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Scaling a figure larger or smaller from a center point by a scale factor, changing size but not shape.

concepts.title: Geometric constructions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building figures such as perpendicular bisectors, angle bisectors, and equilateral triangles using only a compass and straightedge.

concepts.title: Symmetry
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Line (reflective) and rotational symmetry of figures, where a transformation maps the figure onto itself.

concepts.title: Composing transformations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying a sequence of transformations and finding the single transformation equivalent to the composition.

concepts.title: Polygon angle sums
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The interior angles of an n-gon sum to (n-2)·180 degrees, and exterior angles sum to 360 degrees.

concepts.title: Angle relationships
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Complementary, supplementary, vertical, and adjacent angle relationships, and angles formed by parallel lines and a transversal.

concepts.title: Triangle angle sum
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The interior angles of any triangle sum to 180 degrees, with an exterior-angle corollary.

concepts.title: Line and angle proofs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Writing logical proofs about angles and parallel lines using definitions, postulates, and the transitive property.

concepts.title: Congruent triangle criteria
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, and HL conditions that guarantee two triangles are congruent.

concepts.title: Triangle congruence proofs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Two-column or paragraph proofs establishing that triangles are congruent and using CPCTC.

concepts.title: Triangle inequality theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The sum of any two side lengths of a triangle must exceed the third side, constraining which triangles can exist.

concepts.title: Properties of quadrilaterals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining properties of parallelograms, rectangles, rhombi, squares, and trapezoids.

concepts.title: Bisectors and triangle centers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Perpendicular bisectors, angle bisectors, medians, and altitudes and the centers (circumcenter, incenter, centroid) they form.

concepts.title: Similar triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Triangles with equal angles and proportional sides, established by AA, SAS, or SSS similarity.

concepts.title: Proportionality in triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Theorems such as the triangle proportionality (side-splitter) theorem arising from parallel lines cutting triangles.

concepts.title: Solving problems with similar figures
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using proportions from similar figures to find missing lengths, including indirect measurement and scale drawings.

concepts.title: The Pythagorean theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: In a right triangle, a² + b² = c², relating the legs to the hypotenuse.

concepts.title: Right triangle trig ratios (SOH-CAH-TOA)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining sine, cosine, and tangent as ratios of sides in a right triangle.

concepts.title: Special right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The fixed side ratios of 45-45-90 and 30-60-90 triangles.

concepts.title: Solving right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using trig ratios and inverse trig to find unknown sides and angles in right triangles, including angles of elevation and depression.

concepts.title: Area and perimeter on the coordinate plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding the perimeter and area of polygons whose vertices are given as coordinates.

concepts.title: Scale factor and area/volume ratios
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: When a figure is scaled by factor k, its area scales by k² and its volume by k³.

concepts.title: Distance and midpoint formulas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the distance between and midpoint of two points using coordinates.

concepts.title: Dividing line segments
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding a point that partitions a segment in a given ratio using coordinates.

concepts.title: Parallel & perpendicular lines on the coordinate plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using slope criteria to write equations of lines parallel or perpendicular to a given line through a point.

concepts.title: Proofs with parallelograms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using coordinates and slope/distance to prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram, rectangle, or other special figure.

concepts.title: Equation of a circle
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The standard form (x-h)² + (y-k)² = r² describing a circle by center and radius.

concepts.title: Ellipses and hyperbolas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Standard-form equations and key features (foci, axes) of ellipses and hyperbolas.

concepts.title: Parabolas as conic sections
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining a parabola by its focus and directrix and deriving its equation.

concepts.title: Triangle similarity in right triangles (geometric mean)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The altitude to the hypotenuse creates similar sub-triangles, giving geometric-mean relationships among the segments.

concepts.title: Arcs, central and inscribed angles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Relationships between arc measures, central angles, and inscribed angles in a circle.

concepts.title: Arc length and sector area
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing arc length and sector area as fractions of a circle's circumference and area, including radian intuition.

concepts.title: Inscribed shapes and cyclic quadrilaterals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Properties of polygons inscribed in a circle, including that opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral are supplementary.

concepts.title: Tangents, chords, and secants
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Properties of tangent lines, chords, and secants of a circle and the angle and length relationships among them.

concepts.title: Surface area of solids
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing the total surface area of prisms, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres from their nets.

concepts.title: Area of triangles and polygons
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Formulas for the area of triangles, parallelograms, trapezoids, and regular polygons.

concepts.title: Volume of prisms and cylinders
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing volume as base area times height for prisms and cylinders.

concepts.title: Volume of pyramids, cones, and spheres
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Volume formulas for pyramids, cones (one-third base times height), and spheres (4/3 π r³).

concepts.title: Cavalieri's principle
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Two solids with equal cross-sectional areas at every height have equal volume, justifying oblique-solid volume formulas.

concepts.title: Density and 3D modeling
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying volume and surface-area formulas to real objects, including density (mass per unit volume) problems.

concepts.title: Cross-sections and solids of revolution
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The 2D shapes formed by slicing a solid and the 3D solids generated by rotating a 2D region.

concepts.title: Adding and subtracting polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Combining polynomials by adding or subtracting like terms across multiple variables and degrees.

concepts.title: Degree, terms, and standard form of polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Classifying polynomials by degree and number of terms and writing them in descending standard form.

concepts.title: Multiplying polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Distributing every term of one polynomial across another, generalizing FOIL to higher degrees.

concepts.title: Special polynomial products
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Patterns like (a+b)³ and sum/difference of cubes used to expand efficiently.

concepts.title: Average rate of change
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The change in a function's output over an interval divided by the change in input, the slope of a secant line.

concepts.title: The imaginary unit i
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining i = √-1 and the cyclic powers of i.

concepts.title: Arithmetic with complex numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Adding, subtracting, multiplying, and dividing complex numbers using conjugates.

concepts.title: Completing the square in Algebra 2
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Completing the square to solve quadratics with complex solutions and to derive the quadratic formula.

concepts.title: The complex plane
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Representing complex numbers as points or vectors with real and imaginary axes, including modulus.

concepts.title: Modulus and absolute value of complex numbers
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The distance of a complex number from the origin in the complex plane, computed as the square root of the sum of squares.

concepts.title: Factoring higher-degree polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Factoring by grouping, factoring out common factors, and using sum/difference of cubes.

concepts.title: Factor and remainder theorems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Relating a polynomial's roots to its factors and using the remainder when dividing by (x-a).

concepts.title: The fundamental theorem of algebra
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A degree-n polynomial has exactly n complex roots counting multiplicity, with non-real roots occurring in conjugate pairs.

concepts.title: Polynomial long division
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Dividing one polynomial by another using a long-division algorithm.

concepts.title: Polynomial division by quadratics
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using long division to divide a polynomial by a quadratic or higher-degree divisor.

concepts.title: Synthetic division
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: A shorthand procedure for dividing a polynomial by a linear factor (x-a).

concepts.title: Rational root theorem
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Listing candidate rational roots of a polynomial as ratios of factors of the constant and leading coefficient.

concepts.title: End behavior of polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How the leading term's degree and sign determine the graph's behavior as x approaches infinity.

concepts.title: Zeros and multiplicity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How the multiplicity of each root governs whether a graph crosses or touches the x-axis.

concepts.title: Positive and negative intervals of polynomials
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Determining where a polynomial is above or below the x-axis using its zeros and a sign chart.

concepts.title: Graphing polynomial functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sketching polynomial graphs from zeros, multiplicity, end behavior, and turning points.

concepts.title: Polynomial identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Verifying and using algebraic identities such as the difference of squares and Pascal's triangle for binomial expansion.

concepts.title: Rational exponents
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Interpreting fractional exponents as roots and applying exponent rules to them.

concepts.title: Simplifying and rationalizing radicals
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Simplifying radical expressions and rationalizing denominators.

concepts.title: Adding and multiplying radical expressions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Combining like radicals and multiplying radical expressions, including binomials with radicals.

concepts.title: Solving radical equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving equations containing radicals by isolating and raising to a power, checking for extraneous solutions.

concepts.title: Graphing radical functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The shape and domain restrictions of square-root and cube-root function graphs and their transformations.

concepts.title: Exponential growth and decay models
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building and interpreting exponential models, including continuous growth with the number e.

concepts.title: Comparing linear and exponential growth
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Contrasting constant additive growth with constant multiplicative growth and seeing why exponential eventually dominates.

concepts.title: Introduction to logarithms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The logarithm as the inverse of exponentiation: log_b(x) answers 'b to what power gives x?'.

concepts.title: Properties of logarithms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The product, quotient, power, and change-of-base rules for logarithms.

concepts.title: Solving exponential and logarithmic equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using logs and exponentials as inverse operations to solve equations with unknown exponents.

concepts.title: Logarithmic scales
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How logarithmic scales (pH, decibels, Richter) compress large ranges of values into manageable units.

concepts.title: Graphs of logarithmic functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The shape of y = log_b(x) as the reflection of an exponential graph across y = x, with its vertical asymptote.

concepts.title: Evaluating logarithms
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing logarithms by rewriting them as exponential equations, including common logs (base 10).

concepts.title: The natural logarithm
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The logarithm base e (ln) and its role in continuous growth and solving equations involving e^x.

concepts.title: Transformations of functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Shifting, stretching, compressing, and reflecting graphs via changes to the function rule.

concepts.title: Transforming absolute value and square root graphs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying shifts, stretches, and reflections to parent absolute-value and square-root functions.

concepts.title: Even and odd functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Symmetry of functions about the y-axis (even) or origin (odd) and how to test for it.

concepts.title: Inverse functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Functions that undo each other, found by swapping inputs and outputs and reflecting across y = x.

concepts.title: Composing functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Forming a new function f(g(x)) by feeding the output of one function into another.

concepts.title: Modeling with piecewise functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Building and graphing piecewise functions to model situations with different rules over different intervals.

concepts.title: Rational functions and asymptotes
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Graphing ratios of polynomials, identifying vertical, horizontal, and slant asymptotes and holes.

concepts.title: Solving rational equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Solving equations with rational expressions by clearing denominators and checking for extraneous roots.

concepts.title: Direct and inverse variation
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Relationships where one quantity is a constant multiple of another (direct) or of its reciprocal (inverse).

concepts.title: Quadratics with complex roots
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the quadratic formula when the discriminant is negative to find complex conjugate roots.

concepts.title: Simplifying rational expressions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reducing, multiplying, dividing, adding, and subtracting fractions whose numerators and denominators are polynomials.

concepts.title: Systems of nonlinear equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding intersection points of a line with a circle or parabola, or two conics, by substitution.

concepts.title: The unit circle and radians
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Extending sine and cosine to all angles using coordinates on the unit circle, measured in radians.

concepts.title: Radian measure
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring angles as arc length per radius and converting between degrees and radians.

concepts.title: Trig values of special angles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The exact sine, cosine, and tangent values at common angles like 30, 45, and 60 degrees from the unit circle.

concepts.title: Graphing sine and cosine
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Sketching sinusoidal graphs and identifying amplitude, period, phase shift, and midline.

concepts.title: The Pythagorean trig identity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Deriving sin²θ + cos²θ = 1 from the unit circle and using it to find one ratio from another.

concepts.title: Constructing models from data and constraints
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Translating verbal descriptions, tables, and constraints into equations, inequalities, or function models.

concepts.title: Interpreting features of models in context
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Explaining what intercepts, maxima, asymptotes, and rates of change mean in a real-world model.

concepts.title: Choosing a function model
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Selecting linear, quadratic, exponential, or other function families to fit and model real-world data.

concepts.title: Pythagorean theorem review
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Reviewing a² + b² = c² as the foundation for right-triangle trigonometry.

concepts.title: Sine, cosine, and tangent ratios
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining the three primary trig ratios from the sides of a right triangle (SOH-CAH-TOA).

concepts.title: Finding all ratios from one trig value
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Given one trig ratio, reconstructing the right triangle to find the remaining five trig ratios.

concepts.title: Reciprocal trig ratios
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Cosecant, secant, and cotangent as reciprocals of sine, cosine, and tangent.

concepts.title: Inverse trig in right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using arcsine, arccosine, and arctangent to find unknown angles from side ratios.

concepts.title: Right triangle word problems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Setting up and solving multi-step real-world problems by drawing and labeling right triangles.

concepts.title: Modeling with right triangles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying trig to angle-of-elevation, depression, and other real-world right-triangle problems.

concepts.title: Cofunction and complementary angle identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The relationship sin θ = cos(90° - θ) and other cofunction identities arising from complementary angles in a right triangle.

concepts.title: The unit circle
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining sine and cosine as the y- and x-coordinates of a point on the unit circle for any angle.

concepts.title: Radians and degree conversion
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Measuring angles as arc length on the unit circle and converting between radians and degrees.

concepts.title: Graphs of sine and cosine
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Tracing the unit circle into the wave-shaped graphs of sine and cosine.

concepts.title: Amplitude, period, and phase shift
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How coefficients in y = a·sin(b(x-c))+d stretch, compress, and shift a sinusoidal graph.

concepts.title: Graphs of tangent and reciprocal functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The periodic graphs of tangent, cotangent, secant, and cosecant and their asymptotes.

concepts.title: Exact values at special angles
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The exact sine, cosine, and tangent values at 30°, 45°, 60°, and their multiples around the unit circle.

concepts.title: Reference angles and signs by quadrant
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Using the acute reference angle and the quadrant to determine the value and sign of a trig function for any angle.

concepts.title: Inverse trigonometric functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Defining arcsine, arccosine, and arctangent as functions with restricted domains so they pass the vertical line test.

concepts.title: Midline and vertical translation of trig graphs
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: How the constant d in a sinusoid raises or lowers the midline about which the wave oscillates.

concepts.title: Modeling with sinusoidal functions
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Fitting sine or cosine functions to periodic phenomena like tides, daylight hours, or oscillation.

concepts.title: Law of sines
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The relationship a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C used to solve non-right triangles.

concepts.title: Law of cosines
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The generalization c² = a² + b² - 2ab·cos C used to solve triangles given SAS or SSS.

concepts.title: Bearings and navigation problems
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Applying the laws of sines and cosines to real-world navigation, surveying, and bearing problems.

concepts.title: Area of a triangle using trig
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Computing triangle area as (1/2)ab·sin C from two sides and the included angle.

concepts.title: The ambiguous case (SSA)
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: When the law of sines is given SSA, there may be zero, one, or two possible triangles.

concepts.title: The Pythagorean identity
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: The identity sin²θ + cos²θ = 1, derived from the unit circle.

concepts.title: Angle sum and difference identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities for sin(A±B), cos(A±B), and tan(A±B) used to rewrite combined angles.

concepts.title: Double-angle and half-angle identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities for sin(2θ), cos(2θ), and half-angle expressions derived from the sum formulas.

concepts.title: Reciprocal and quotient identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities expressing tangent as sine over cosine and the reciprocal relationships among the six trig functions.

concepts.title: Product-to-sum and sum-to-product formulas
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Identities that convert products of sines and cosines into sums and vice versa, useful for simplifying expressions.

concepts.title: Proving trigonometric identities
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Transforming one side of a proposed identity using known identities until it matches the other side.

concepts.title: Solving trigonometric equations
concepts.subject: math
concepts.description: Finding all angle solutions to equations involving trig functions, using the unit circle and periodicity.
